titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Elizabeth Mayer and Wolfgang Sauerländer Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 4309652.
This collection contains 1 published digital object. And that object are comprised of 152 digital files.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday January 9, 2025 at 01:56

did.unittitle: Elizabeth Mayer and Wolfgang Sauerländer papers

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection consists of letters to Elizabeth Mayer and Wolfgang Sauerländer concerning their translation and editorial work, with related reports and legal documents relating to business operations of Pantheon Books, Random House, and the Bollingen Foundation. Many letters concern Mayer's and Sauerländer's personal and professional relationships with Kurt and Helen Wolff. Other correspondence includes letters to Sauerländer from Jacques Schriffrin, letters and documents relating to translations of Bertolt Brecht by Ralph Manheim, and letters to Mayer from Norman Holmes Pearson and Erich and Fine Kahler, accompanied by photographs of Fine Kahler. Most correspondence to Sauerländer includes typescript carbon copies of his responses.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Beate Sauerländer on the Beinecke German Literature Collection Fund, 1997.
June 2023 Acquisition: Purchased using the Edith and Richard French Fund, 2023.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four groupings: I. Letters to Elizabeth Mayer, 1933-1966. II. Wolfgang Sauerländer Correspondence, 1953-1977. III. Other Papers, 1956-1972. IV. June 2023 Acquisition, 1892-2022.

bioghist.head: Wolfgang Sauerländer
bioghist.p: Wolfgang Sauerländer (1911-1977), editor, employed by Pantheon Books and the Bollingen Foundation. Sauerländer was married to Beate Sauerländer, daughter of Elizabeth Mayer.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Elizabeth Mayer and Wolfgang Sauerländer Papers. Yale Collection of German Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Former call number: Uncat Zg Ms 14. This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization, in 2011. The current finding aid supercedes a preliminary list created in 1997. Folder titles and content notes are based on a vendor description and have not been verified in all cases against contents of folders.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of letters to Elizabeth Mayer and Wolfgang Sauerländer concerning their translation and editorial work, with related reports and legal documents relating to business operations of Pantheon Books, Random House, and the Bollingen Foundation. Many letters concern Mayer's and Sauerländer's personal and professional relationships with Kurt and Helen Wolff. Other correspondence includes letters to Sauerländer from Jacques Schriffrin, letters and documents relating to translations of Bertolt Brecht by Ralph Manheim, and letters to Mayer from Norman Holmes Pearson and Erich and Fine Kahler, accompanied by photographs of Fine Kahler. Most correspondence to Sauerländer includes typescript carbon copies of his responses.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Elizabeth Mayer and Wolfgang Sauerländer Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased using the Edith and Richard French Fund, 2023.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This accession is arranged in four groupings: I. Wolfgang Sauerländer papers, 1929-2007. II. Elizabeth and William Mayer papers, 1898-2022. III. Josef Scharl papers, 1936-2000. IV. Helen Hokinson drawings, 1949.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This accession contains materials related to the life and work of Elizabeth Mayer, William Mayer, Wolfgang Sauerländer, Josef Scharl, and Helen Hokinson. Includes correspondence, notes, writings, personal and family papers, photographs, printed material, and artwork.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 autograph letter, signed, from Erich Kahler, 1948
6 typed letters, signed, fom Fine Kahler, 1933-1945, with photographs of Fine Kahler

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 35 letters, including 1 photocopy, and 5 postcards. Early letters relate to Wolff's experiences in France after fleeing from Italy, his efforts to immigrate, and the political situation. Later letters contain comments about Mayer's collaboration with W. H. Auden.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 autograph letters, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: About Sauerländer's employment with the Bollingen Series

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With related printed materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 typed letters, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: About Sauerländer's work for Random House on Ralph Manheim's Brecht translation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters and memos, with copies of letters from Sauerländer, about Pantheon Books

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 typed letter, signed, with copies of 3 letters from Sauerländer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 6 letters, with copies of many letters from Sauerländer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 24 letters and memos, about indexing and editing done by Sauerländer, with copies of many letters from Sauerländer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 18 letters and memos, about Pantheon Books and the Bollingen Series, with clippings and copies of letters from Sauerländer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 11 letters and memos, with copies of letters from Sauerländer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 32 letters and memos, from Wolff at Locarno, with copies of many letters from Sauerländer, showing how Wolff ran his business from a distance

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 autograph letters, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copy of an agreement between Pantheon Books and the Bollingen Foundation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copy of an account of a European scouting trip by Kurt Wolff, about his visits to authors, artists, and publishers, typescript carbon, 21 pages

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents relating to Sauerländer's work as a consultant on the Random House Brecht translation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains correspondence, photographs, and other documents to, by, or relating to Wolfgang Sauerländer.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series maintains order found at time of acquisition.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 23: Restricted until 2027. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Richard Beer-Hofmann was an Austrian dramatist and poet.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sarah Blackburn was a writer, married at one time to Paul Blackburn, and one of two witnesses to Wolfgang's marriage to Beata Mayer Wachstein.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Anthony Banner was a Spanish translator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kappo Phelan Brooks was a writer who was at Yaddo and on staff at Pantheon Books.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gertrude Buckman was a secretary for Kurt Wolff at Pantheon Books in the 1940s and former spouse of Delmore Schwartz.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Maud Chow worked with Wolfgang Sauerländer at Bollingen Foundation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Jules Cohn was the Director of Economic & Manpower Corporation, a deptartment of Research & Development at Bollingen Foundation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Maxine Cronbach worked at Church Peace Union.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Paul Faerber was an architect and Beata Mayer/Wachstein/Sauerländer's biological father. Mouche Faerber was Paul Faerber's wife. Renate Faerber was journalist and married to Paul and Mouche's son Peer-Uli.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Allen Forbes was the founder of the Haydn Society.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Harry Ford was the head of production and poetry editor at Atheneum Publishers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Janet Glover worked on the index of Collected Works of Jung, published by Bollingen Foundation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Otto Gombosi was a musicologist married to Anne Gombosi. Anne Gombosi was first violinist for the Seattle String Quartet and friend of Wolfgang Sauerländer and Josef Scharl.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Martin Green was an English professor at Tufts University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Haydn Soceity was organized in 1949 to publish the complete works of Joseph Haydn and to record his music and other composers who either influenced him or were influenced by him.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Georg Hirschfeld was a German playwright.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: R.F.C. Hull was a prolific translator, especially of Carl Jung.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Anne Marie Jauss was an illustrator and author of books for children.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lotte Jacobi was a photographer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uwe Johnson was a German writer, editor, and scholar.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Erich Kahler was a scholar, author, critic, and husband of Fine Kahler.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fine Kahler was the wife of Erich Kahler.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Anthony and Elaine Kerrigan were Spanish translators associated with Bollingen Foundation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Phyllis Lehmann was an archaeologist.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mary Manheim was a French translator, and wife of Ralph Manheim.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ralph Manheim was a French and German translator, and married to Mary Manheim.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Paul Thomas Mann was a German writer, social critic, philanthropist, and 1929 Nobel laureate in Literature.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Josef Marx was an Oboist, musicologist, and music publisher.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Jerry V. McGill was a former Hunter College professor in psychology and philosophy, musician, and husband to artist Helen Ludwig. He was dismissed from Hunter College for being communist in 1954.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: McGinnis & Marx Publishers were a music publishing company.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: William (Bill) McGuire was an editor, scholar, author, and married to Paula McGuire.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Paula McGuire was an editor at Pantheon Books, and married to Bill McGuire.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Meridian Books is a paperback publishing company.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: New Directions was a publishing company.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Aldo Olschki was an Italian publisher of the publishing house Leo. S. Olschki.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hilda Pertha was an artist in Mendocino, CA., and former spouse of Beryl Sokoloff.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gary Schwartz was a publishing company in Amsterdam.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Alfons Simon was a German educator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Nicolas Slonimsky was a composer, conductor and lexicographer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Beryl Sokoloff was a filmmaker, photographer and painter, and married to Hilda Sokoloff. Hilda Sokoloff (nee Pertha) was an artist.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: James Stern was an author and translator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Barbara Sterne was a friend to Wolfgang Sauerländer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: George Thmpson was an illustrator and graphic designer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kenneth Tigar was a translator and actor.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Edith Tschichold was an influential typographer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Regina Ulimann was an Austrian writer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Oswald Garrison Villard was the owner of and writer for New York Evening Post and the Nation, a pacifist, a civil rights advocate, and sponsor of Wolfgang Sauerländer's visa extensions to remain in US.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Larry Wallrich was the founder of the Phoenix Book Store in New York, and a book seller in London and Toronto.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: International book publishers and founders of Pantheon Books.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Peter Yates was a musicologist, music critic, and author.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged into three subseries: I. Correspondence, 1901-2022. II. Translations, 1926-2013. III. Personal papers, 1898-2013.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This sub-series contains correspondence and accompanying material sent to and from Elizabeth and William Mayer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wystan Hugh (W.H.) Auden was a British-American poet and co-translator with Elizabeth Mayer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Esther Bailey cleaned for the Mayers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Charlie (Charlotte) Baxter was a friend of Basil Douglas and the Kahlers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sybille Bedford was an English writer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Arnold Benfey was a German pediatrician.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bob Black was a rare book dealer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Louise Bogan was an American poet and co-translator with Elizabeth Mayer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Elsa Brandstrom-Ulrich was a Swedish nurse and philanthropist.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Esther (Esti) Brunn was married to Dr. Fritz Brunn.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tapia Caballero was a Chilean pianist.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Heinrich Campendonk was a German/Dutch expressionist painter.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Porter Ralph Chandler was an authority on constitutional law, and married to Gabrielle Chandler.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Theodore Chandler was an American composer and music critic.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Genevieve Chaudhuri was a cellist with the Pittsburgh Symphony.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Father Damascus was a Benedictine monk.

did.unittitle: Davis Family - Elizabeth and Beata Sauerländer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Richard Hallock Davis was an American diplomat and Ambassador to Romania. Catherine (Cate) Davis is Richard Hallock's sister, and Blanche Davis is their aunt.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ida Dehmel was a German lyric poet and muse, a feminist, a supporter of the arts, and friend of Stefan George.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Margaret DeSilver was the treasurer of the Evelyn Scott Fund.

did.unittitle: Douglas, Basil - Elizabeth Mayer, Beata Sauerländer, Muki Fairchild

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Basil Douglas was a Scottish musician and music administrator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Louis Dubertret was a geologist and engineer who worked in the Middle East, and a nephew of Jean Bloé Niestle, the Swiss painter.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Alfred Einstein was a German musicologist and editor of the first major revision of the Köchel catalogue.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Robert Forster-Larringa was a German actor and assistant director who worked in silent films.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Joseph Freeman was an American writer, editor of the New Masses, and founding editor of the Partisan Review.

did.unittitle: Gaupp, Drs. Robert and Vera - William Mayer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dr. Robert Gaupp was a German psychiatrist, mentor of William Mayer, and was worked with leading psychiatrists of his day, i.e. Wernicke, Bonhoeffer, Kraepelin, and father to Vera Gaupp. Dr. Vera Gaupp was a German pediatrician and piano student of Elizabeth Mayer as a child.

did.unittitle: Gropper, Joseph - Elizabeth Mayer, Beata Sauerländer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Joseph Gropper was an art dealer in Massachusetts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Frieda Hallswachs-Zerny was a singer and close friend of Hugo Wolf.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gillian Hansard was an English student who took German lessons in Munich.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Adolf Hölzel was a German expressionist painter.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lotte Jacobi was a German photographer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Else Jaffe was an early German social scientist and sister-in-law of D.H. Lawrence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Richard Johns was an editor of Pagany.

did.unittitle: Kahler, Josephine (Fine) - Correspondence, William and Elizabeth Mayer
Kahler, Josephine (Fine) - Correspondence from Beata Mayer Wachstein
Kienesberger, Konrad F. - Beata Sauerländer/Elizabeth Mayer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Konrad F. Kienesberger was an Austrian editor and publisher.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Masa Kitakawa was a Japanese pianist.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rudolf Laudenheimer was a German psychiatrist.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Waldemar Lessing was an art historian. Elisabeth Lessing was married to Waldemar. Ursula Lessing was Waldemar and Elisabeth's daughter.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Alfred Lewisohn was a rubber and tea merchant. Ina Lewisohn was married to Alfred. Doreen Lewisohn was a paper conservator and Alfred and Ina's daughter.

did.unittitle: Lewisohn, Doreen - Journey to the West

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mary Lindsay was married to a New York City Mayor, John Lindsey.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Caroline Lippincott was a writer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Herta List was a singer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ada Loewith was a musician who played with Elizabeth Mayer in New York City.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: David Luke was an Oxford Don and translator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lavinia Mazzucchetti was an Italian anti-fascist and translator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Frank McClain was an Episcopalian priest, and friend of Michael Mayer.

did.unittitle: Moore, Harry (D.H. Lawrence, Southern Illinois University) - Elizabeth Mayer and Beata Sauerländer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Harry Moore was a professor of English at Southern Illinois University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Marianne Moore was an American poet and co-translator with Elizabeth Mayer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lysbeth Muncy was a historian who specialized in Prussia.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lore Neresheimer was an actress, wife of Paul Neresheimer, and painter who painted a portrait of Michael Mayer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The newspaper articles are photocopies.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Marguerite (Miguette) Niestle was married to Jean Bloé Niestle, the Swiss painter.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Frances Olitsky was married to Peter Olitsky.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Angiolo Orvieto was an Italian author and editor, co-founder of literary review Il Marzocco and founder of Vita Nuova and Nazione Letteraria.

did.unittitle: Pauer, Max (Conservatory in Stuttgart/Concerts) - Elizabeth Mayer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Max Pauer was director of the Royal Conservatory of Music in Stuttgart and Elizabeth Mayer's piano professor.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Norman Pearson (Yale 1932) was a Yale professor of English and American studies.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reginold Phelps was a Harvard historian who specialized in Germany.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Godfrey Pilkington was a British art dealer and director of the Piccadilly Gallery.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Luise Pistorius was Elizabeth Mayer's closest friend from childhood.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chaim Raphael was a scholar and novelist who wrote under the name Jocelyn Davey.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Louise Rogers was married to a teacher at Christopher Mayer's school.

did.unittitle: Ruoff, Selma and Margar to Elizabeth Mayer and Beata Sauerländer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Selma Ruoff was a German botanist.

did.unittitle: Russell, Haide and Ulrich von Ramm "The Prince"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Haide Russel was on the Counsel for Cultural Affairs at the Embassy of the Federal Republic of Germany. Ulrich von Ramm was a diplomat.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Isabella Saunders was a translator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: May Sarton was a Belgian-American poet.

did.unittitle: Sauerländer, J.D. [Johann David] to Elizabeth Mayer and William Mayer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Johann David Sauerländer was Wolfgang Sauerländer's father, a lawyer and scholar.

did.unittitle: Sauerländer, Wolfgang to Elizabeth and William Mayer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wolfgang Sauerländer was a lawyer and editor who left Germany in 1939 with Josef Scharl when both were in the German army.

did.unittitle: Sauerländer, Johann David and Matilda, vol.1
Sauerländer, Johann David and Matilda, vol.2
Sauerländer, Johann David and Matilda, vol.3
Sauerländer, Johann David and Matilda - Elizabeth Mayer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kurt Seelman was a teacher in Munich and married to Herta Seelman.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Alfons Simons was a German school reformer in Bavaria and married to Hilde Simons.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Una Smyth was a British novelist who wrote under the name Marius Lyle.

did.unittitle: Stern, James and Tania - Elizabeth Mayer/William Mayer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: James Stern was a Anglo-Irish writer, translator, and married to Tania Stern. Tania Stern was a physical therapist and co-translator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wolfgang Stopler was an economist and married to "Vögli" Stopler.

did.unittitle: Van de Kamp, Olga and Peter

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Peter Van de Kamp was a Dutch astronomer, musician, and married to Olga Van de Kamp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ludwig and Margaret Von Hessen were the Prince and Princess of the Rhine.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lewis Metcalf Walling was a New Deal administrator and married to Frances Walling.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: John Wheelock was a poet, chief editor of "Scribner" and married to Phyllis Wheelock. Phyllis Wheelock was the daughter of Charles de Kay.

did.unittitle: Zimmer, Christiane von Hoffmanstal and Heinrich

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Christiane von Hoffmanstal Zimmer was a social scientist. Heinrich Zimmer was a German indologist, linguist, historian of Asian art, and married to Christiane.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains translations, correspondence, articles, and other materials relating to translating in relation to Elizabeth Mayer's profession as a translator.

did.unittitle: Partial list of translations by Elizabeth Mayer
Farrar, Straus and Cudahy, Inc. Publishers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Paul Frankl was an Austrian art historian.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bruno Cigognani was a realist writer and playwright.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cesare Giordino was a writer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Alberto Moravia was a journalist and novelist.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dino Provenzal was a writer and teacher.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dino Traverso was a writer, painter, poet, and journalist.

did.unittitle: Italian to German translations - G.A. Borghese, Dino Provenzal, Bonaventura Tecchi, Federigo Tozzi

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Giovanni Verga was an Italian realist writer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ernst Jünger was a German philosopher, author, and entomologist.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Marie Luise Kaschnitz was a German short story writer, essayist and poet.

did.unittitle: Verlag, Ernst Klegg - Bedford: Favorite of the Gods
Read, Conyers and Dr. Hermann Rinn

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Conyers Read was an American historian specializing in history of England. Hermann Rinn was a German publisher.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Henry Regnery was a publisher of several translated works done by Elizabeth Mayer.

did.unittitle: Schlipp, Paul - Library of Living Philosophers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Paul Schlipp was an educator and editor of the Library of Living Philosophers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This sub-series contains correspondence, photographs, diaries, and documents relating to Elizabeth and William Mayers' personal lives. Featured are substantial communication between Elizabeth (Wolff) Mayer and Paul Faerber and between Elizabeth and William Mayer.

did.unittitle: Early life - Elizabeth Mayer up to 1912
"The Midwest Calendar for 1942" and "The New England Calendar for Engagements"
Paul Faerber and H.F. - Elizabeth Mayer
Paul Faerber and M.F. - Elizabeth Mayer and Beata Mayer Wachstein
Beata Mayer Wachstein - obituary for Paul Faerber
"Die Madonna vom Grödner Tal" by Eine Novelle

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains correspondence, artwork, and ephemera to, by, or relating to Josef "Sepp" Scharl.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series maintains order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: Josef Scharl - Elizabeth and William Mayer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains black and white sketches, color drawings, and correspondence of Helen Hokinson, New Yorker artist and cartoonist.
